## PR #11: Refactor agents

**Status**: Open  
**Changes**: +781 / -783 across 24 files

### Key Changes

| File | Changes | Description |
|------|---------|-------------|
| `src/app/api/agents/v1/route.ts` | +98 / -420 | Major simplification of the API route |
| `src/lib/agents/creative-agent.ts` | +60 | New agent definition |
| `src/lib/agents/index.ts` | +3 | Agent exports |
| `src/lib/agents/middleware/error-handler.ts` | +75 | New error handling middleware |
| `src/lib/agents/middleware/tracing.ts` | +86 | New tracing middleware |
| `src/lib/agents/tools/*` | +229 | Individual tool files (modular structure) |
| `src/lib/agents/utils/stream-utils.ts` | +86 | Stream utility functions |

### Deleted Files
- `src/lib/agents/model-suggester/model.md` (-72)
- `src/lib/agents/model-suggester/tools.ts` (-221)

### Structure
The refactor moves from a monolithic structure to a modular one with:
- Separate tool files (`image-generation.ts`, `video-generation.ts`, etc.)
- Middleware layer (error-handler, tracing)
- Centralized agent definition (`creative-agent.ts`)

{"additions":448,"body":"This pull request refactors the Media Agent's architecture for clarity and maintainability, standardizes tool and action naming, and improves the agent API integration with the UI and database. It also updates documentation and developer guidance, and fixes a critical bug where tool outputs were lost after page refresh. The most important changes are grouped below:

**Media Agent Architecture & Documentation:**
- Added a comprehensive architecture overview and troubleshooting guide for the Media Agent in `.claude/CLAUDE.md`, including tech stack, file responsibilities, agent flow, available tools, client integration, and a detailed post-mortem/fix for the tool parts persistence bug.

**Agent API & Tool Integration:**
- Refactored `src/app/api/agents/v1/route.ts` to use the new `mediaAgent` and `mediaAgentTools` from `src/lib/agents/media-agent`, replacing the previous ad-hoc tools registry and system prompt logic with a unified agent call options pattern. The endpoint now uses `createAgentUIStreamResponse` for streaming and error handling. **Tool & Action Naming Consistency:**
- Standardized tool and action names across the codebase and documentation, e.g., `generateImageWithPollingTool` → `generateImageTool`, `bgRemoverTool` → `removeBackgroundTool`, etc. Updated all references in rules, registry, and UI rendering logic to use the new names. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-82bfde0abf3fca8fb7db27f7f1e6ecab6ab9bf9df2603ad5261071f64b315ac5L64-R74) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-82bfde0abf3fca8fb7db27f7f1e6ecab6ab9bf9df2603ad5261071f64b315ac5L109-R115) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-5fcd1251a83ee6d06e80ae85420c7aaad45fb8a6aebaac4c12b1614121d10edeL92-R96) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-5fcd1251a83ee6d06e80ae85420c7aaad45fb8a6aebaac4c12b1614121d10edeL273-R279) [[5]](diffhunk://#diff-5fcd1251a83ee6d06e80ae85420c7aaad45fb8a6aebaac4c12b1614121d10edeL425-R431) [[6]](diffhunk://#diff-34a753b9af20918f6d4fedcc6c66d154502921076ab4423a24c961a56172f9faL7-R13)

**Bug Fixes & Developer Experience:**
- Documented and fixed a bug where tool outputs (e.g., generated images) were lost after page refresh due to improper merging of message parts in the database. Added a `mergeParts` function and updated developer debugging instructions.
- Updated developer instructions to use `pnpm` instead of other package managers in `README.md` and `.cursor/rules/notes.mdc`. +++ b/.claude/CLAUDE.md

 ### Framework-Specific Guidance

+
 - Use Next.js `<Image>` component for images
 - Use `next/head` or App Router metadata API for head elements
 - Use Server Components for async data fetching instead of async Client Components
 
 **React 19+:**
+
 - Use ref as a prop instead of `React.forwardRef`
 
 **Solid/Svelte/Vue/Qwik:**
+
 - Use `class` and `for` attributes (not `className` or `htmlFor`)
 
 ---
@@ -121,3 +124,167 @@ Biome's linter will catch most issues automatically. Focus your attention on:
 ---
 
 Most formatting and common issues are automatically fixed by Biome. Run `npx ultracite fix` before committing to ensure compliance.
+
+---
+
+# AI Agent Architecture
+
+## Overview
+
+This project includes a **Media Agent** - an AI-powered creative assistant for image generation, video creation, audio production, and media editing. It's built using the **Vercel AI SDK v6+** with the `ToolLoopAgent` pattern.
+
+## Tech Stack
+
+- **AI SDK**: `ai` (v6.0.1), `@ai-sdk/anthropic`, `@ai-sdk/openai`, `@ai-sdk/react`
+- **Model**: Claude Haiku 4.5 (`claude-haiku-4-5`) via Anthropic
+- **Observability**: Langfuse for tracing and prompt management
+- **Database**: PostgreSQL with Drizzle ORM for chat persistence
+
+## Key Files
+
+| File                                                                    | Purpose                                                     |
+| ----------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- |
+| `src/app/api/agents/v1/route.ts`                                        | Main agent API endpoint using `createAgentUIStreamResponse` |
+| `src/lib/agents/media-agent/index.ts`                                   | Agent definition with `ToolLoopAgent`                       |
+| `src/lib/agents/media-agent/types.ts`                                   | Agent call options schema (auto/manual mode)                |
+| `src/lib/agents/model-suggester/tools.ts`                               | Tool definitions (image gen, video gen, etc.)               |
+| `src/lib/agents/model-suggester/prompts.ts`                             | System prompts (auto mode, manual mode)                     |
+| `src/components/product/editor/agent/ChatClient.tsx`                    | Client-side `useChat` hook integration                      |
+| `src/components/product/editor/agent/MessagePartRenderer.tsx`           | Renders message parts (text, tools, media)                  |
+| `src/components/product/editor/agent/CustomToolOutput.tsx`              | Renders tool outputs with polling                           |
+| `src/server/database/repo/postgress-repo-functions/chatMessage.repo.ts` | Chat message persistence                                    |
+
+## Architecture Flow
+
+```
+User Input → ChatClient (useChat) → API Route (/api/agents/v1)
+                                           ↓
+                                   createAgentUIStreamResponse
+                                           ↓
+                                   ToolLoopAgent (mediaAgent)
+                                           ↓
+                              Tool Execution (generateImage, etc.)
+                                           ↓
+                              Stream Response → Client Rendering
+                                           ↓
+                              onFinish → Save to PostgreSQL
+```
+
+## Agent Configuration
+
+The `mediaAgent` uses:
+
+- **Model**: `anthropic("claude-haiku-4-5")`
+- **Max Output Tokens**: 10,000
+- **Stop Condition**: `stepCountIs(20)` - max 20 tool loop iterations
+- **Dynamic Prompts**: `prepareCall` hook injects system prompt based on auto/manual mode
+
+## Available Tools
+
+| Tool               | Description                                 |
+| ------------------ | ------------------------------------------- |
+| `generateImage`    | Generate images with client-side polling    |
+| `generateVideo`    | Generate videos with polling                |
+| `editImage`        | Edit images using AI (uses nano_banana_pro) |
+| `generateAudio`    | Text-to-speech generation                   |
+| `removeBackground` | AI background removal                       |
+| `resizeImage`      | AI-powered image resizing                   |
+| `upscaleImage`     | Image upscaling (x2, x4)                    |
+
+## Client-Side Integration
+
+The `ChatClient.tsx` uses:
+
+- `useChat` hook from `@ai-sdk/react`
+- `DefaultChatTransport` for API communication
+- `sendAutomaticallyWhen: lastAssistantMessageIsCompleteWithToolCalls` for multi-step tool execution
+- `onFinish` callback for canvas integration (adding generated media)
+
+## Message Part Types
+
+Tool parts use the AI SDK v6+ typed format:
+
+- `type: "tool-{toolName}"` (e.g., `"tool-generateImage"`)
+- `state`: `"input-streaming"` | `"input-available"` | `"output-available"` | `"output-error"`
+- `input`: Tool input parameters
+- `output`: Tool execution result
+- `toolCallId`: Unique identifier for the tool call
+
+---
+
+# Known Issues & Fixes
+
+## Issue: Tool Parts Lost After Page Refresh (Fixed Feb 2026)
+
+### Problem
+
+When using `createAgentUIStreamResponse` with `ToolLoopAgent`, multi-step tool executions caused tool parts to be overwritten in the database.
+
+**Symptom**: Images and credit info showed during streaming but disappeared after page refresh.
+
+### Root Cause
+
+The agent executes in multiple steps:
+
+1. **Step 1**: Tool execution (e.g., `tool-generateImage`) - saved to DB
+2. **Step 2**: Text response (continuation) - **OVERWROTE** step 1 data
+
+Both steps used the same `continuationMessageId`, and the upsert in `saveChatMessage` was **replacing** parts instead of **merging** them.
+
+### Solution
+
+Modified `chatMessage.repo.ts` to **merge parts** instead of replacing:
+
+```typescript
+// Before: Parts were replaced
+const metadata = { parts: message.parts };
+
+// After: Parts are merged with existing
+const existingMessage = await db.select()...;
+if (existingMessage.length > 0) {
+  finalParts = mergeParts(existingParts, newParts);
+}
+```
+
+The `mergeParts` function:
+
+- Uses `toolCallId` to identify unique tool parts (no duplicates)
+- Keeps only one `step-start` part
+- Keeps the final `text` response with `state: "done"`
+- Sorts: step-start → tool parts → text parts
+
+### Files Changed
+
+- `src/server/database/repo/postgress-repo-functions/chatMessage.repo.ts` - Added `mergeParts` function
+
+### Testing
+
+After the fix, generate an image/use a tool, then refresh the page. The tool output (credits, image placeholder) should persist.
+
+---
+
+## Debugging Agent Issues
+
+### Enable Debug Logs
+
+Add these to see message flow:
+
+```typescript
+// In MessagePartRenderer.tsx
+console.log("[DEBUG] parts:", JSON.stringify(parts, null, 2));
+
+// In chatMessage.repo.ts (saveChatMessage)
+console.log("[DEBUG] saving parts:", JSON.stringify(message.parts, null, 2));
+
+// In route.ts (onFinish)
+console.log(
+  "[DEBUG] responseMessage:",
+  JSON.stringify(responseMessage, null, 2)
+);
+```
+
+### Common Issues
+
+1. **Tool not rendering**: Check if `part.type` matches switch cases in `MessagePartRenderer.tsx`
+2. **Parts missing after refresh**: Check if parts are being merged correctly in `saveChatMessage`
+3. **Credits not showing**: Verify `part.output.totalCredits` exists and `state === "output-available"`

@@ -121,3 +124,167 @@ Biome's linter will catch most issues automatically. Focus your attention on:
 ---
 
 Most formatting and common issues are automatically fixed by Biome. Run `npx ultracite fix` before committing to ensure compliance.
+
+---
+
+# AI Agent Architecture
+
+## Overview
+
+This project includes a **Media Agent** - an AI-powered creative assistant for image generation, video creation, audio production, and media editing. It's built using the **Vercel AI SDK v6+** with the `ToolLoopAgent` pattern.
+
+## Tech Stack
+
+- **AI SDK**: `ai` (v6.0.1), `@ai-sdk/anthropic`, `@ai-sdk/openai`, `@ai-sdk/react`
+- **Model**: Claude Haiku 4.5 (`claude-haiku-4-5`) via Anthropic
+- **Observability**: Langfuse for tracing and prompt management
+- **Database**: PostgreSQL with Drizzle ORM for chat persistence
+
+## Key Files
+
+| File                                                                    | Purpose                                                     |
+| ----------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- |
+| `src/app/api/agents/v1/route.ts`                                        | Main agent API endpoint using `createAgentUIStreamResponse` |
+| `src/lib/agents/media-agent/index.ts`                                   | Agent definition with `ToolLoopAgent`                       |
+| `src/lib/agents/media-agent/types.ts`                                   | Agent call options schema (auto/manual mode)                |
+| `src/lib/agents/model-suggester/tools.ts`                               | Tool definitions (image gen, video gen, etc.)               |
+| `src/lib/agents/model-suggester/prompts.ts`                             | System prompts (auto mode, manual mode)                     |
+| `src/components/product/editor/agent/ChatClient.tsx`                    | Client-side `useChat` hook integration                      |
+| `src/components/product/editor/agent/MessagePartRenderer.tsx`           | Renders message parts (text, tools, media)                  |
+| `src/components/product/editor/agent/CustomToolOutput.tsx`              | Renders tool outputs with polling                           |
+| `src/server/database/repo/postgress-repo-functions/chatMessage.repo.ts` | Chat message persistence                                    |
+
+## Architecture Flow
+
+```
+User Input → ChatClient (useChat) → API Route (/api/agents/v1)
+                                           ↓
+                                   createAgentUIStreamResponse
+                                           ↓
+                                   ToolLoopAgent (mediaAgent)
+                                           ↓
+                              Tool Execution (generateImage, etc.)
+                                           ↓
+                              Stream Response → Client Rendering
+                                           ↓
+                              onFinish → Save to PostgreSQL
+```
+
+## Agent Configuration
+
+The `mediaAgent` uses:
+
+- **Model**: `anthropic("claude-haiku-4-5")`
+- **Max Output Tokens**: 10,000
+- **Stop Condition**: `stepCountIs(20)` - max 20 tool loop iterations
+- **Dynamic Prompts**: `prepareCall` hook injects system prompt based on auto/manual mode
+
+## Available Tools
+
+| Tool               | Description                                 |
+| ------------------ | ------------------------------------------- |
+| `generateImage`    | Generate images with client-side polling    |
+| `generateVideo`    | Generate videos with polling                |
+| `editImage`        | Edit images using AI (uses nano_banana_pro) |
+| `generateAudio`    | Text-to-speech generation                   |
+| `removeBackground` | AI background removal                       |
+| `resizeImage`      | AI-powered image resizing                   |
+| `upscaleImage`     | Image upscaling (x2, x4)                    |
+
+## Client-Side Integration
+
+The `ChatClient.tsx` uses:
+
+- `useChat` hook from `@ai-sdk/react`
+- `DefaultChatTransport` for API communication
+- `sendAutomaticallyWhen: lastAssistantMessageIsCompleteWithToolCalls` for multi-step tool execution
+- `onFinish` callback for canvas integration (adding generated media)
+
+## Message Part Types
+
+Tool parts use the AI SDK v6+ typed format:
+
+- `type: "tool-{toolName}"` (e.g., `"tool-generateImage"`)
+- `state`: `"input-streaming"` | `"input-available"` | `"output-available"` | `"output-error"`
+- `input`: Tool input parameters
+- `output`: Tool execution result
+- `toolCallId`: Unique identifier for the tool call
+
+---
+
+# Known Issues & Fixes
+
+## Issue: Tool Parts Lost After Page Refresh (Fixed Feb 2026)
+
+### Problem
+
+When using `createAgentUIStreamResponse` with `ToolLoopAgent`, multi-step tool executions caused tool parts to be overwritten in the database.
+
+**Symptom**: Images and credit info showed during streaming but disappeared after page refresh.
+
+### Root Cause
+
+The agent executes in multiple steps:
+
+1. **Step 1**: Tool execution (e.g., `tool-generateImage`) - saved to DB
+2. **Step 2**: Text response (continuation) - **OVERWROTE** step 1 data
+
+Both steps used the same `continuationMessageId`, and the upsert in `saveChatMessage` was **replacing** parts instead of **merging** them.
+
+### Solution
+
+Modified `chatMessage.repo.ts` to **merge parts** instead of replacing:
+
+```typescript
+// Before: Parts were replaced
+const metadata = { parts: message.parts };
+
+// After: Parts are merged with existing
+const existingMessage = await db.select()...;
+if (existingMessage.length > 0) {
+  finalParts = mergeParts(existingParts, newParts);
+}
+```
+
+The `mergeParts` function:
+
+- Uses `toolCallId` to identify unique tool parts (no duplicates)
+- Keeps only one `step-start` part
+- Keeps the final `text` response with `state: "done"`
+- Sorts: step-start → tool parts → text parts
+
+### Files Changed
+
+- `src/server/database/repo/postgress-repo-functions/chatMessage.repo.ts` - Added `mergeParts` function
+
+### Testing
+
+After the fix, generate an image/use a tool, then refresh the page. The tool output (credits, image placeholder) should persist.
+
+---
+
+## Debugging Agent Issues
+
+### Enable Debug Logs
+
+Add these to see message flow:
+
+```typescript
+// In MessagePartRenderer.tsx
+console.log("[DEBUG] parts:", JSON.stringify(parts, null, 2));
+
+// In chatMessage.repo.ts (saveChatMessage)
+console.log("[DEBUG] saving parts:", JSON.stringify(message.parts, null, 2));
+
+// In route.ts (onFinish)
+console.log(
+  "[DEBUG] responseMessage:",
+  JSON.stringify(responseMessage, null, 2)
+);
+```
+
+### Common Issues
+
+1. **Tool not rendering**: Check if `part.type` matches switch cases in `MessagePartRenderer.tsx`
+2. **Parts missing after refresh**: Check if parts are being merged correctly in `saveChatMessage`
+3. **Credits not showing**: Verify `part.output.totalCredits` exists and `state === "output-available"`

content.text:
Now I have a clear picture. Let me provide a detailed comparison:

## Architecture Comparison

| Aspect | PR #11 (Teammate) | PR #19 (Yours) |
|--------|-------------------|----------------|
| **Structure** | Highly modular - 7 separate tool files | Consolidated - tools in `media-agent/index.ts` |
| **Middleware** | Dedicated `error-handler.ts` & `tracing.ts` | Inline in route.ts with Langfuse |
| **Agent name** | `creativeAgent` | `mediaAgent` |
| **AI SDK** | Upgraded to `v6.0.62` | Keeps `v6.0.1` |
| **Documentation** | Minimal | Extensive (CLAUDE.md with architecture, debugging, bug fixes) |
| **Bug fixes** | None | Tool parts persistence fix |
| **Files changed** | 24 files (+781/-783) | 15 files (+448/-228) |

## PR #11 Strengths
- **Granular file structure** - Each tool in its own file (easier to find/edit one tool)
- **Abstracted middleware** - Reusable error handling and tracing
- **Updated AI SDK** - Latest version (6.0.62)
- **Clean exports** via `src/lib/agents/index.ts`

## PR #19 (Yours) Strengths
- **ToolLoopAgent pattern** - Uses `createAgentUIStreamResponse` properly
- **Comprehensive documentation** - Architecture, debugging guides, known issues
- **Bug fix included** - Tool parts persistence fix (critical for UX)
- **Smaller change surface** - Less risk of breaking things
- **Standardized naming** - `generateImage`, `removeBackground` etc.

## My Recommendation

**Merge PR #19 (yours) first**, then have the teammate rebase PR #11 on top. Here's why:

1. **Critical bug fix** - Your PR fixes the tool parts persistence issue which affects production UX
2. **Foundation** - Your `ToolLoopAgent` + `createAgentUIStreamResponse` is the correct AI SDK v6+ pattern
3. **Documentation** - Your CLAUDE.md provides invaluable context for future development
4. **Lower risk** - Fewer file changes, easier to review

**Then for PR #11:**
- Keep the modular tool files structure (can be adopted on top of yours)
- Keep the middleware abstractions
- Adopt the AI SDK upgrade
- But align with your naming (`mediaAgent`, `generateImage`, etc.)

diff --git a/src/lib/agents/creative-agent.ts b/src/lib/agents/creative-agent.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..c1102ef
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/lib/agents/creative-agent.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,60 @@
+/**
+ * Creative Agent
+ *
+ * A ToolLoopAgent specialized for creative media generation tasks.
+ * Uses call options to dynamically set instructions based on mode.
+ */
+
+import { anthropic } from "@ai-sdk/anthropic";
+import { ToolLoopAgent } from "ai";
+import { z } from "zod";
+import { mainAgentPrompt, manualModeAgentPrompt } from "./prompts";
+import {
+  bgRemoverTool,
+  GenerateEditImageTool,
+  generateAudioTool,
+  generateImageWithPollingTool,
+  generateVideoWithPollingTool,
+  imageResizerTool,
+  imageUpscalerTool,
+} from "./tools";
+
+// Define call options schema for dynamic behavior
+const callOptionsSchema = z.object({
+  isManualMode: z.boolean(),
+  imageModel: z.string().nullable(),
+  videoModel: z.string().nullable(),
+  globalSystemInstructions: z.string().optional(),
+});
+
+export type CreativeAgentCallOptions = z.infer<typeof callOptionsSchema>;
+
+export const creativeAgent = new ToolLoopAgent({
+  model: anthropic("claude-haiku-4-5"),
+  instructions: mainAgentPrompt,
+  tools: {
+    generateImageWithPolling: generateImageWithPollingTool,
+    generateVideoWithPolling: generateVideoWithPollingTool,
+    GenerateEditImage: GenerateEditImageTool,
+    generateAudio: generateAudioTool,
+    bgRemover: bgRemoverTool,
+    imageResizer: imageResizerTool,
+    imageUpscaler: imageUpscalerTool,
+  },
+  callOptionsSchema,
+  prepareCall: ({ options, ...settings }) => {
+    // Build dynamic instructions based on mode
+    const basePrompt = options.isManualMode
+      ? manualModeAgentPrompt(options.imageModel, options.videoModel)
+      : mainAgentPrompt;
+
+    const mergedInstructions = options.globalSystemInstructions
+      ? `${options.globalSystemInstructions}\n\n${basePrompt}`
+      : basePrompt;
+
+    return {
+      ...settings,
+      instructions: mergedInstructions,
+    };
+  },
+});

1→import { anthropic } from "@ai-sdk/anthropic";
     2→import { type InferAgentUIMessage, stepCountIs, ToolLoopAgent } from "ai";
     3→
     4→import {
     5→  removeBackgroundTool,
     6→  editImageTool,
     7→  generateAudioTool,
     8→  generateImageTool,
     9→  generateVideoTool,
    10→  resizeImageTool,
    11→  upscaleImageTool,
    12→} from "@/lib/agents/model-suggester/tools";
    13→import {
    14→  main_agent_prompt,
    15→  manual_mode_agent_prompt,
    16→} from "@/lib/langfuse/prompt";
    17→import { getPromptWithFallback } from "@/lib/langfuse/prompt-fetcher";
    18→import logger from "@/utils/logger";
    19→
    20→import {
    21→  type MediaAgentCallOptions,
    22→  mediaAgentCallOptionsSchema,
    23→} from "./types";
    24→
    25→/**
    26→ * Tools available to the media agent.
    27→ * Exported for validation in the route handler.
    28→ */
    29→export const mediaAgentTools = {
    30→  generateImage: generateImageTool,
    31→  generateVideo: generateVideoTool,
    32→  editImage: editImageTool,
    33→  generateAudio: generateAudioTool,
    34→  removeBackground: removeBackgroundTool,
    35→  resizeImage: resizeImageTool,
    36→  upscaleImage: upscaleImageTool,
    37→};
    38→
    39→/**
    40→ * Media Agent - A specialized AI creative assistant for image generation,
    41→ * video creation, audio production, and media editing tasks.
    42→ *
    43→ * Uses ToolLoopAgent pattern for cleaner architecture and built-in loop control.
    44→ */
    45→export const mediaAgent = new ToolLoopAgent<
    46→  MediaAgentCallOptions,
    47→  typeof mediaAgentTools
    48→>({
    49→  id: "media-agent",
    50→  model: anthropic("claude-haiku-4-5"),
    51→  tools: mediaAgentTools,
    52→  maxOutputTokens: 10_000,
    53→  stopWhen: stepCountIs(20),
    54→  callOptionsSchema: mediaAgentCallOptionsSchema,
    55→  experimental_telemetry: { isEnabled: true },
    56→
    57→  /**
    58→   * Prepare the call with dynamic system prompt based on mode (auto vs manual).
    59→   * Receives default settings and returns overrides for this call.
    60→   * IMPORTANT: Must pass through `prompt` from input to output (SDK uses prompt, not messages).
    61→   */
    62→  prepareCall: async ({ options, model, tools, maxOutputTokens, prompt }) => {
    63→    const isManualMode = options && !options.isAutoMode;
    64→
    65→    let baseSystemPrompt: string;
    66→
    67→    if (isManualMode && options) {
    68→      // Manual mode: use user's selected models
    69→      const manualPromptFallback = manual_mode_agent_prompt(
    70→        options.imageModel,
    71→        options.videoModel,
    72→      );
    73→      baseSystemPrompt = await getPromptWithFallback(
    74→        "manual_mode_agent_prompt",
    75→        manualPromptFallback,
    76→      );
    77→      logger.info(
    78→        `Manual mode enabled - Image: ${options.imageModel}, Video: ${options.videoModel}`,
    79→      );
    80→    } else {
    81→      // Auto mode: agent selects best models
    82→      baseSystemPrompt = await getPromptWithFallback(
    83→        "main_agent_prompt",
    84→        main_agent_prompt,
    85→      );
    86→      logger.info("Auto mode enabled - agent will select best models");
    87→    }
    88→
    89→    // Merge with user's custom system instructions if provided
    90→    const globalInstructions = options?.globalSystemInstructions?.trim();
    91→    const mergedInstructions = [
    92→      baseSystemPrompt,
    93→      globalInstructions?.length
    94→        ? `\n\n<user-system-instructions>\n${globalInstructions}\n</user-system-instructions>\n`
    95→        : "",
    96→    ].join("");
    97→
    98→    // Return the updated settings - pass through required fields including prompt
    99→    // NOTE: createAgentUIStreamResponse passes model messages as `prompt`, not `messages`
   100→    return {
   101→      model,
   102→      tools,
   103→      maxOutputTokens,
   104→      instructions: mergedInstructions,
   105→      prompt, // CRITICAL: Must pass through prompt from createAgentUIStreamResponse
   106→    };
   107→  },
   108→});
   109→
   110→/**
   111→ * Type-safe UIMessage type for the media agent.
   112→ * Use this with useChat<MediaAgentUIMessage>() for type-safe tool results.
   113→ */
   114→export type MediaAgentUIMessage = InferAgentUIMessage<typeof mediaAgent>;
   115→
   116→// Re-export types for convenience
   117→export type { MediaAgentCallOptions } from "./types";
   118→

1→import { z } from "zod";
     2→
     3→/**
     4→ * Schema for media agent call options.
     5→ * These options control how the agent behaves for each call.
     6→ */
     7→export const mediaAgentCallOptionsSchema = z.object({
     8→  /**
     9→   * When true, the agent automatically selects the best image/video models.
    10→   * When false, uses the user's manually selected models.
    11→   */
    12→  isAutoMode: z.boolean().default(true),
    13→
    14→  /**
    15→   * The image model to use when isAutoMode is false.
    16→   * Ignored when isAutoMode is true.
    17→   */
    18→  imageModel: z.string().nullable().default(null),
    19→
    20→  /**
    21→   * The video model to use when isAutoMode is false.
    22→   * Ignored when isAutoMode is true.
    23→   */
    24→  videoModel: z.string().nullable().default(null),
    25→
    26→  /**
    27→   * Optional custom system instructions provided by the user.
    28→   * These are appended to the base system prompt.
    29→   */
    30→  globalSystemInstructions: z.string().optional(),
    31→});
    32→
    33→/**
    34→ * Type for media agent call options, inferred from the Zod schema.
    35→ */
    36→export type MediaAgentCallOptions = z.infer<typeof mediaAgentCallOptionsSchema>;
    37→

1→// tools/auto-model-suggest.ts
     2→import {
     3→  bgRemoverAction,
     4→  generateAudioAction,
     5→  GenerateEditImageAction,
     6→  generateImageResizerAction,
     7→  generateImageUpscalerAction,
     8→  generateImageWithPollingAction,
     9→  generateVideoWithPollingAction,
    10→} from "@/server/actions/ai.actions"; // 'use server' fn
    11→import { tool } from "ai";
    12→import { z } from "zod";
    13→
    14→
    15→
    16→// [tool] Generate image with polling (create + client-side poll)
    17→export const generateImageTool = tool({
    18→  description:
    19→    "Generate an image using the specified model. Creates the generation request and returns immediately. The image will be polled client-side and added to the canvas when ready.",
    20→  inputSchema: z.object({
    21→    prompt: z
    22→      .string()
    23→      .describe("The text prompt describing the image to generate"),
    24→    modelName: z
    25→      .string()
    26→      .describe('The model ID to use for generation (e.g., "imagen-4")'),
    27→    aspect_ratio: z
    28→      .string()
    29→      .optional()
    30→      .describe(
    31→        'Image aspect ratio (e.g., "1:1", "16:9", "9:16"). Defaults to "1:1"',
    32→      ),
    33→  }),
    34→  execute: async ({ prompt, modelName, aspect_ratio = "1:1" }) => {
    35→    const res = await generateImageWithPollingAction({
    36→      prompt,
    37→      modelName,
    38→      aspect_ratio,
    39→    });
    40→    return res;
    41→  },
    42→});
    43→
    44→// [tool] Generate video with polling (create + poll + return)
    45→export const generateVideoTool = tool({
    46→  description:
    47→    'Generate a video using the specified model. This handles the complete workflow: creates the generation, polls for completion, and returns the final result. The final output contains the predictionId and requestLink, return a custom video element in the markdown like [BUNDLED##{"type": "video", "data": {"predictionId": "{{ predictionId }}", "requestLink": "{{ requestLink }}"}}](https://bundled.design/)',
    48→  inputSchema: z.object({
    49→    prompt: z
    50→      .string()
    51→      .describe("The text prompt describing the video to generate"),
    52→    modelName: z
    53→      .string()
    54→      .describe('The video model ID to use for generation'),
    55→    image_url: z
    56→      .string()
    57→      .optional()
    58→      .describe("Optional input image URL for image-to-video generation"),
    59→    aspect_ratio: z
    60→      .string()
    61→      .optional()
    62→      .describe(
    63→        'Video aspect ratio (e.g., "16:9", "9:16", "1:1"). Defaults to "16:9"',
    64→      ),
    65→    duration: z
    66→      .string()
    67→      .optional()
    68→      .describe(
    69→        'Video duration (e.g., "5", "8s"). If omitted, sensible defaults are applied per model.',
    70→      ),
    71→    resolution: z
    72→      .string()
    73→      .optional()
    74→      .describe(
    75→        'Video resolution (e.g., "720p", "1080p"). If omitted, sensible defaults are applied per model.',
    76→      ),
    77→    negative_prompt: z
    78→      .string()
    79→      .optional()
    80→      .describe("Optional negative prompt to avoid certain artifacts."),
    81→    generate_audio: z
    82→      .boolean()
    83→      .optional()
    84→      .default(true)
    85→      .describe("Whether to generate audio with the video. Defaults to true. Required for VEO 3.1 and similar models."),
    86→  }),
    87→  execute: async ({
    88→    prompt,
    89→    modelName,
    90→    image_url,
    91→    aspect_ratio = "16:9",
    92→    duration,
    93→    resolution,
    94→    negative_prompt,
    95→    generate_audio = true,
    96→  }) => {
    97→    const res = await generateVideoWithPollingAction({
    98→      prompt,
    99→      modelName,
   100→      image_url,
   101→      aspect_ratio,
   102→      duration,
   103→      resolution,
   104→      negative_prompt,
   105→      generate_audio,
   106→    });
   107→    return res;
   108→  },
   109→});
   110→
   111→// [tool] Generate edited image(s) using smart-edit v4 API with polling (multi-image support)
   112→export const editImageTool = tool({
   113→  description:
   114→    "Edit one or multiple images using the smart-edit v4 API. Supports up to 5 images at once. CRITICAL: ALWAYS use 'nano_banana_pro' as the model - this is the ONLY image editing model available. This handles the complete workflow: creates the edit request, polls for completion, and returns the final result.",
   115→  inputSchema: z.object({
   116→    prompt: z
   117→      .string()
   118→      .describe("The text prompt describing how to edit the image(s)"),
   119→    image_urls: z
   120→      .array(z.string())
   121→      .min(1, "At least one image URL is required")
   122→      .max(5, "Maximum 5 images allowed")
   123→      .describe("Array of image URLs to edit (1-5 images)"),
   124→    model_name: z
   125→      .literal("nano_banana_pro")
   126→      .default("nano_banana_pro")
   127→      .describe(
   128→        "MUST be 'nano_banana_pro' - this is the ONLY editing model. Do NOT use seedream-4.5 or any other model for editing.",
   129→      ),
   130→  }),
   131→  execute: async ({ prompt, image_urls, model_name }) => {
   132→    const res = await GenerateEditImageAction({
   133→      prompt,
   134→      image_urls,
   135→      model_name,
   136→    });
   137→    return res;
   138→  },
   139→});
   140→
   141→// [tool] Generate audio using TTS model
   142→export const generateAudioTool = tool({
   143→  description:
   144→    "Generate audio using text-to-speech (TTS) model. Creates high-quality voice audio from text prompts.",
   145→  inputSchema: z.object({
   146→    prompt: z.string().describe("The text to convert to speech"),
   147→  }),
   148→  execute: async ({ prompt }) => {
   149→    const res = await generateAudioAction({ prompt });
   150→    return res;
   151→  },
   152→});
   153→
   154→// [tool] Remove background from images using AI
   155→export const removeBackgroundTool = tool({
   156→  description:
   157→    "Remove background from images using AI-powered background removal. Creates the processing request and returns immediately. The result will be polled client-side and added to the canvas when ready.",
   158→  inputSchema: z.object({
   159→    imageUrl: z
   160→      .string()
   161→      .describe("The URL of the image to remove background from"),
   162→  }),
   163→  execute: async ({ imageUrl }) => {
   164→    const res = await bgRemoverAction({ imageUrl });
   165→    return res;
   166→  },
   167→});
   168→
   169→// [tool] Resize images with AI-powered quality enhancement
   170→export const resizeImageTool = tool({
   171→  description:
   172→    "Resize images with AI-powered quality enhancement. Creates the processing request and returns immediately. The result will be polled client-side and added to the canvas when ready.",
   173→  inputSchema: z.object({
   174→    imageUrl: z.string().describe("The URL of the image to resize"),
   175→    aspect_ratio: z
   176→      .enum(["1:1", "16:9", "9:16"])
   177→      .default("1:1")
   178→      .describe("The desired aspect ratio. Defaults to '1:1'"),
   179→    quality: z
   180→      .string()
   181→      .optional()
   182→      .describe(
   183→        "The quality level ('high', 'medium', 'low'). Defaults to 'high'",
   184→      ),
   185→  }),
   186→  execute: async ({ imageUrl, aspect_ratio, quality = "high" }) => {
   187→    const res = await generateImageResizerAction({
   188→      imageUrl,
   189→      aspect_ratio,
   190→      quality,
   191→    });
   192→    return res;
   193→  },
   194→});
   195→
   196→
   197→export const upscaleImageTool = tool({
   198→  description:
   199→    "Upscale images with AI-powered quality enhancement. Creates the processing request and returns immediately. The result will be polled client-side and added to the canvas when ready.",
   200→  inputSchema: z.object({
   201→    imageUrl: z.string().describe("The URL of the image to resize"),
   202→    upscale_factor: z
   203→      .enum(["x2", "x4"]) // backend expects values like x2/x4
   204→      .describe("The desired upscale factor (e.g., 'x2', 'x4')"),
   205→    compression_quality: z
   206→      .number()
   207→      .int()
   208→      .min(1)
   209→      .max(100)
   210→      .optional()
   211→      .describe("The compression quality level (1-100). Defaults to 100"),
   212→  }),
   213→  execute: async ({ imageUrl, upscale_factor, compression_quality }) => {
   214→    const res = await generateImageUpscalerAction({
   215→      imageUrl,
   216→      upscale_factor,
   217→      compression_quality,
   218→    }); // compression_quality is optional
   219→    return res;
   220→  },
   221→});
   222→

1→
     2→export const vision_prompt: string = `
     3→You are an expert video model router. Your task is to select the best video model for a user's request.
     4→
     5→IMPORTANT: Always return a BASE model ID. The backend will automatically resolve to the correct variant (text-to-video or image-to-video) based on whether an image is provided.
     6→
     7→Available BASE Models (use these exact IDs):
     8→
     9→| Model ID | Best For |
    10→|----------|----------|
    11→| veo3.1 | Highest cinematic realism, production-grade quality (DEFAULT) |
    12→| veo3.1/fast | Rapid iteration, quick drafts |
    13→| bytedance/seedance/v1/pro | Multi-shot narratives, anime/illustration style |
    14→| minimax/hailuo-2.3/pro | Dynamic motion, dance, sports, action |
    15→| sora-2 | OpenAI's video model, creative content |
    16→| kling-video/v2.6/pro | High quality, stable motion |
    17→| luma-dream-machine/ray-2 | Cinematic camera, 3D rendering |
    18→
    19→Routing guidance:
    20→- Cinematic hero shots / brand films → veo3.1
    21→- Fast drafts or variations → veo3.1/fast
    22→- Multi-scene stories or anime → bytedance/seedance/v1/pro
    23→- Dance, sports, dynamic motion → minimax/hailuo-2.3/pro
    24→- General creative content → sora-2 or veo3.1/fast
    25→
    26→Rules:
    27→- Return ONLY valid JSON. No code fences.
    28→- Use BASE model ID only (NOT /text-to-video or /image-to-video suffix)
    29→- Keep reason concise (under 20 words)
    30→
    31→Example:
    32→{"model_id":"veo3.1","reason":"Cinematic quality for brand film."}
    33→`;
    34→
    35→export const main_agent_prompt: string = `You are a SPECIALIZED AI creative assistant designed for image generation, video creation, audio production, media editing tasks, and PDF document analysis for creative content generation.
    36→
    37→IMPORTANT BOUNDARIES - SAFEGUARDS:
    38→- I handle: image generation, video creation, audio generation, image editing, background removal, resizing, upscaling, AND PDF document analysis for creative content
    39→- I DO NOT engage in: general conversation, coding help, math problems, trivia, writing assistance (unless related to media/PDF content)
    40→- If asked about anything outside my scope, I politely decline: "I'm specialized for creative media tasks and PDF analysis. Please ask me to generate images, videos, audio, edit media, or analyze PDF documents for creative content."
    41→
    42→TOOL CALL LIMITATIONS - WARNING:
    43→- Maximum 3 tool calls per step
    44→- NEVER generate 3+ images/videos in one request
    45→- If user asks for 10+ items, respond: "I can only generate 1-2 high-quality results at a time."
    46→- For complex requests, break them into multiple steps
    47→
    48→PROMPT ENHANCEMENT - CRITICAL:
    49→When calling generation tools, ALWAYS enhance the user's prompt:
    50→- Add descriptive details: lighting, colors, mood, style, composition, quality terms
    51→- Transform basic prompts into detailed, high-quality prompts
    52→- Keep the core intent but make it more specific and vivid
    53→
    54→IMAGE MODEL SELECTION (Choose directly - NO autoModelSuggest needed):

    57→| Model ID | Best For |

    59→| flux-2-flex | Text/typography, memes, posters, logos with text, infographics, UI mockups |
    60→| nano-banana-pro | Educational content, diagrams, data visualization, multi-reference compositions |
    61→| seedream-4.5 | Character consistency, product photography, cinematic, fashion, general creative (DEFAULT) |
    62→| imagen-4 | Google's advanced model, detailed images, good quality |
    63→| flux-2-pro | Highest quality Flux model |
    64→
    65→Quick Decision:
    66→- Has readable text/typography? → flux-2-flex
    67→- Is infographic/educational/diagram? → nano-banana-pro
    68→- Everything else (portraits, products, creative, cinematic) → seedream-4.5
    69→
    70→For IMAGE GENERATION (Direct call - skip autoModelSuggest):

    72→  prompt: "ENHANCED detailed prompt",
    73→  modelName: "flux-2-flex" | "nano-banana-pro" | "seedream-4.5",
    74→  aspect_ratio: "1:1" | "16:9" | "9:16"
    75→})
    76→
    77→For VIDEO GENERATION:
    78→═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    79→⚠️ ALWAYS use BASE model ID. Backend auto-resolves text-to-video vs image-to-video!
    80→
    81→Available Video Models (BASE IDs only):
    82→| Model ID | Best For |
    83→|----------|----------|
    84→| veo3.1 | Cinematic realism (DEFAULT) |
    85→| veo3.1/fast | Quick drafts |
    86→| bytedance/seedance/v1/pro | Anime, stylized |
    87→| minimax/hailuo-2.3/pro | Dance, sports, motion |
    88→| sora-2 | Creative content |
    89→═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    90→
    91→For TEXT-TO-VIDEO (no image provided):
    92→generateVideo({
    93→  prompt: "ENHANCED detailed prompt",
    94→  modelName: "veo3.1",  // Use BASE model ID
    95→  aspect_ratio: "16:9" | "9:16" | "1:1"
    96→})
    97→
    98→For IMAGE-TO-VIDEO (user provides an image):

   100→  prompt: "ENHANCED detailed prompt",

   104→})
   105→
   106→For IMAGE EDITING/PROCESSING (direct calls):

content.text:
Perfect! I've analyzed the route file thoroughly. Let me create a comprehensive report of my findings.

## Analysis Report: `/src/app/api/agents/v1/route.ts`

### 1. **Current Imports**

The file imports from these key areas:

**Observability & Tracing:**
- `@langfuse/tracing` (observe, updateActiveObservation, updateActiveTrace)
- `@opentelemetry/api` (trace)
- `../../../../../instrumentation` (langfuseSpanProcessor)

**AI SDK:**
- `ai` (createAgentUIStreamResponse, createUIMessageStream, createUIMessageStreamResponse, TypeValidationError, validateUIMessages, UIMessage)

**Framework:**
- `next/server` (NextRequest, after)

**Authentication & Database:**
- `@/lib/auth/utils` (authenticateUser)
- `@/lib/agents/media-agent` (mediaAgent, mediaAgentTools, MediaAgentCallOptions)
- `@/server/database/repo/postgress-repo-functions/chat.repo` (createChat, getChatById)
- `@/server/database/repo/postgress-repo-functions/chatMessage.repo` (loadChatMessages, saveChatMessage)
- `@/server/database/repo/postgress-repo-functions/imageEditorProjects.repo` (findProjectByIdForUser)
- `@/utils/logger` (logger)

---

### 2. **Key Functions & Utilities Used**

| Category | Function | Purpose |
|----------|----------|---------|
| **Authentication** | `authenticateUser(requestId)` | Validates user identity from request context |
| **Chat Management** | `getChatById()` / `createChat()` | Retrieve or create chat sessions |
| **Message Persistence** | `loadChatMessages()` / `saveChatMessage()` | Load and persist messages to PostgreSQL |
| **Authorization** | `findProjectByIdForUser()` | Verify user owns the project |
| **Validation** | `validateUIMessages()` | Validate messages against tool schemas |
| **Logging** | `logger.info/warn/error()` | Structured logging for debugging |
| **Tracing** | `updateActiveObservation/Trace()` | Langfuse observability hooks |

---

### 3. **How It Calls the Agent**

**Pattern: `createAgentUIStreamResponse`**

```typescript
return await createAgentUIStreamResponse({
  agent: mediaAgent,                           // Pre-configured ToolLoopAgent
  uiMessages: validatedMessages,              // Validated UI messages array
  options: agentOptions,                      // MediaAgentCallOptions (isAutoMode, modelSelection)
  generateMessageId: () => ...,               // ID persistence for multi-step executions
  onError: (error) => { ... },               // Stream error handler
  onFinish: async ({ responseMessage }) => { ... }  // Post-stream callback
});
```

**Key Features:**
- Streaming up to 180 seconds (`maxDuration = 180`)
- Multi-step tool execution with ID continuity (via `continuationMessageId`)
- Automatic message ID generation with fallback logic
- Error handling before streaming starts

---

### 4. **Inline Helper Functions (Extraction Candidates)**

These 4 utility functions are currently defined at the bottom of the file and could be extracted to a shared module:

| Function | Lines | Purpose | Reusability |
|----------|-------|---------|-------------|
| `extractTextFromUiMessage()` | 395-397 | Get text content from a single message | High - Used in tracing, error handling |
| `extractTextFromParts()` | 399-408 | Extract text from message parts array | High - Helper for above |
| `extractLatestUserTextFromUiMessages()` | 410-420 | Find latest user message's text | Medium - Used for trace input enrichment |
| `summarizeParts()` | 422-432 | Create summary of part types (e.g., "text:1, tool:2") | Medium - Used for logging |

---

### 5. **Request Flow Breakdown**

```
POST /api/agents/v1
  ↓
[1] Authenticate user & get userId
  ↓
[2] Parse request body (message, projectId, chatId, modelSelection)
  ↓
[3] Verify project ownership (findProjectByIdForUser)
  ↓
[4] Validate chatId provided (required)
  ↓
[5] Load or create chat session
  ↓
[6] Load previous messages from database
  ↓
[7] Build messages array (handle user/assistant/continuation messages)
  ↓
[8] Validate messages against tool schemas
  ↓
[9] Save user message to DB (immediate)
  ↓
[10] Build MediaAgentCallOptions
  ↓
[11] Flush Langfuse traces (after() hook)
  ↓
[12] Return createAgentUIStreamResponse
      ├── Agent executes with ToolLoopAgent
      ├── Tools generate media (images, videos, etc.)
      └── Callbacks handle errors and save responses
      
[13] onFinish: Save assistant message to DB
```

---

### 6. **State Management Quirks**

**Continuation Message ID Pattern:**
```typescript
let continuationMessageId: string | undefined;

if (message.role === "assistant") {
  continuationMessageId = message.id;  // Preserve ID for multi-step execution
  messages = previousMessages;         // Use previous, let agent generate new response
}

// Later in generateMessageId:
generateMessageId: () => continuationMessageId || crypto.randomUUID()
```

This enables **upsert-like behavior** where multi-step tool executions (e.g., generateImage → text response) use the same message ID, preventing duplicates in the database.

---

### 7. **Error Handling Strategy**

**Two layers:**

1. **Pre-stream errors** (try-catch around entire handler):
   - Logs error with requestId and stack
   - Creates fallback UI stream with error message
   - Returns friendly message to user

2. **Stream errors** (onError callback):
   - Checks error message for specific conditions (rate limit, timeout, context length)
   - Returns context-aware user message
   - Updates Langfuse observation with ERROR level

---

### 8. **Potential Extraction Opportunities for Refactoring**

The following could be extracted to separate modules:

**Module: `agents/v1/helpers/message-utils.ts`**
- `extractTextFromUiMessage()`
- `extractTextFromParts()`
- `extractLatestUserTextFromUiMessages()`
- `summarizeParts()`

**Module: `agents/v1/helpers/request-validation.ts`**
- Chat resolution logic (lines 79-145)
- Message array building logic (lines 152-172)
- Message validation (lines 180-199)

**Module: `agents/v1/helpers/langfuse-trace.ts`**
- Initial trace setup (lines 64-66, 104-118)
- Error trace updates (lines 335-343)
- Output trace updates (lines 270-287)

**Module: `agents/v1/helpers/error-handler.ts`**
- `onError` callback implementation (lines 228-268)
- Fallback error stream creation (lines 346-361)

---

### 9. **Architecture Readiness**

**Current Structure:**
- Single route handler with ~433 lines
- Multiple responsibilities: validation, persistence, streaming, error handling, tracing
- Four inline utility functions

**Refactoring Opportunities:**
1. Extract message utilities to `lib/agents/shared/message-utils.ts`
2. Extract request validation logic to `lib/agents/v1/validators.ts`
3. Extract error handlers to `lib/agents/v1/error-handlers.ts`
4. Extract Langfuse tracing orchestration to `lib/agents/v1/tracing.ts`
5. Consider separating chat management logic into `lib/agents/v1/chat-manager.ts`

This would reduce the main handler to ~150-200 lines of orchestration logic.


---

### 6. **State Management Quirks**

**Continuation Message ID Pattern:**
```typescript
let continuationMessageId: string | undefined;

if (message.role === "assistant") {
  continuationMessageId = message.id;  // Preserve ID for multi-step execution
  messages = previousMessages;         // Use previous, let agent generate new response
}

// Later in generateMessageId:
generateMessageId: () => continuationMessageId || crypto.randomUUID()
```

This enables **upsert-like behavior** where multi-step tool executions (e.g., generateImage → text response) use the same message ID, preventing duplicates in the database.

---

### 7. **Error Handling Strategy**

**Two layers:**

1. **Pre-stream errors** (try-catch around entire handler):
   - Logs error with requestId and stack
   - Creates fallback UI stream with error message
   - Returns friendly message to user

2. **Stream errors** (onError callback):
   - Checks error message for specific conditions (rate limit, timeout, context length)
   - Returns context-aware user message
   - Updates Langfuse observation with ERROR level

---

### 8. **Potential Extraction Opportunities for Refactoring**

The following could be extracted to separate modules:

**Module: `agents/v1/helpers/message-utils.ts`**
- `extractTextFromUiMessage()`
- `extractTextFromParts()`
- `extractLatestUserTextFromUiMessages()`
- `summarizeParts()`

**Module: `agents/v1/helpers/request-validation.ts`**
- Chat resolution logic (lines 79-145)
- Message array building logic (lines 152-172)
- Message validation (lines 180-199)

**Module: `agents/v1/helpers/langfuse-trace.ts`**
- Initial trace setup (lines 64-66, 104-118)
- Error trace updates (lines 335-343)
- Output trace updates (lines 270-287)

**Module: `agents/v1/helpers/error-handler.ts`**
- `onError` callback implementation (lines 228-268)
- Fallback error stream creation (lines 346-361)

---

### 9. **Architecture Readiness**

**Current Structure:**
- Single route handler with ~433 lines
- Multiple responsibilities: validation, persistence, streaming, error handling, tracing
- Four inline utility functions

**Refactoring Opportunities:**
1. Extract message utilities to `lib/agents/shared/message-utils.ts`
2. Extract request validation logic to `lib/agents/v1/validators.ts`
3. Extract error handlers to `lib/agents/v1/error-handlers.ts`
4. Extract Langfuse tracing orchestration to `lib/agents/v1/tracing.ts`
5. Consider separating chat management logic into `lib/agents/v1/chat-manager.ts`

This would reduce the main handler to ~150-200 lines of orchestration logic.

## Comprehensive Analysis: src/lib/agents Directory Structure

Based on my exploration of the codebase, here's a detailed breakdown of the current implementation:

### 1. **Directory Structure**

```
src/lib/agents/
├── media-agent/
│   ├── index.ts              # Agent definition & exports
│   └── types.ts              # Call options schema & types
└── model-suggester/
    ├── tools.ts              # 7 tool definitions
    ├── prompts.ts            # System prompts (auto & manual modes)
    ├── model.md              # Model configuration docs
    └── utils/
        └── polling.ts        # Prediction polling utility
```

### 2. **Key Files Content Structure**

#### **media-agent/index.ts** (118 lines)
- **Purpose**: Exports the main agent instance and tool collection
- **Exports**:
  - `mediaAgentTools`: Object containing 7 tools (generateImage, generateVideo, editImage, generateAudio, removeBackground, resizeImage, upscaleImage)
  - `mediaAgent`: ToolLoopAgent<MediaAgentCallOptions, typeof mediaAgentTools>
  - `MediaAgentUIMessage`: Type-safe UIMessage type
  - `MediaAgentCallOptions`: Re-export for convenience

- **Agent Configuration**:
  - **Model**: `anthropic("claude-haiku-4-5")`
  - **Max Output Tokens**: 10,000
  - **Stop Condition**: `stepCountIs(20)` - max 20 tool loop iterations
  - **Telemetry**: Enabled (`experimental_telemetry: { isEnabled: true }`)
  - **Schema**: Uses `mediaAgentCallOptionsSchema` for type validation

- **Key Method - prepareCall Hook** (lines 62-107):
  - Receives: `{ options, model, tools, maxOutputTokens, prompt }`
  - Returns: Updated settings with merged instructions
  - Logic:
    - Detects `isManualMode` from options (when user selects models manually)
    - Fetches appropriate prompt from Langfuse or uses fallback
    - **Manual Mode**: Uses `manual_mode_agent_prompt(imageModel, videoModel)`
    - **Auto Mode**: Uses `main_agent_prompt`
    - Merges base prompt with optional `globalSystemInstructions` from user
    - **Critical**: Passes through `prompt` field (AI SDK expects this, not `messages`)

#### **media-agent/types.ts** (37 lines)
- **Purpose**: Schema and type definitions for agent call options
- **mediaAgentCallOptionsSchema** (Zod):
  ```typescript
  {
    isAutoMode: boolean (default: true)
    imageModel: string | null (default: null)
    videoModel: string | null (default: null)
    globalSystemInstructions: string (optional)
  }
  ```
- **MediaAgentCallOptions**: Inferred type from schema

#### **model-suggester/tools.ts** (222 lines)
- **Purpose**: Defines 7 AI tools using Vercel AI SDK `tool()` function
- **Tools**:

  1. **generateImageTool**
     - Calls `generateImageWithPollingAction` (server action)
     - Inputs: prompt, modelName, aspect_ratio (optional)
     - Uses client-side polling pattern

  2. **generateVideoTool**
     - Calls `generateVideoWithPollingAction` (server action)
     - Inputs: prompt, modelName, image_url (optional), aspect_ratio, duration, resolution, negative_prompt, generate_audio
     - Supports both text-to-video and image-to-video
     - Returns predictionId and requestLink

  3. **editImageTool**
     - Calls `GenerateEditImageAction` (server action)
     - Inputs: prompt, image_urls (array, 1-5 images), model_name (literal "nano_banana_pro")
     - Uses smart-edit v4 API with polling

  4. **generateAudioTool**
     - Calls `generateAudioAction` (server action)
     - Inputs: prompt (text for TTS)
     - Simple text-to-speech generation

  5. **removeBackgroundTool**
     - Calls `bgRemoverAction` (server action)
     - Inputs: imageUrl
     - Uses client-side polling pattern

  6. **resizeImageTool**
     - Calls `generateImageResizerAction` (server action)
     - Inputs: imageUrl, aspect_ratio, quality (optional)
     - Uses client-side polling pattern

  7. **upscaleImageTool**
     - Calls `generateImageUpscalerAction` (server action)
     - Inputs: imageUrl, upscale_factor ("x2" | "x4"), compression_quality (optional)
     - Uses client-side polling pattern

- **Key Pattern**: All tools invoke server actions ('use server' functions) and return results immediately (polling happens client-side or within the action)

#### **model-suggester/prompts.ts** (212 lines)
- **Purpose**: System prompts for both auto and manual modes
- **Exports**:

  1. **vision_prompt**: Video model router prompt (34 lines)
     - Routes between 7 video models based on use case
     - Returns JSON: `{ model_id, reason }`

  2. **main_agent_prompt**: Auto-mode prompt (127 lines)
     - Comprehensive guidance for creative tasks
     - **Boundaries**: Image/video/audio generation, media editing, PDF analysis only
     - **Tool Limitations**: Max 3 tool calls per step, never 3+ images/videos in one request
     - **Prompt Enhancement**: Instructs agent to enhance basic prompts with details (lighting, colors, mood, style, composition)
     - **Model Selection Rules**: Detailed routing between 5 image models (flux-2-flex, nano-banana-pro, seedream-4.5, imagen-4, flux-2-pro)
     - **Video Models**: 5 available models with routing logic
     - **Critical Formatting**: "DO NOT include URLs as markdown images - media is auto-added to canvas by UI"

  3. **manual_mode_agent_prompt(imageModel, videoModel)**: Manual-mode prompt (76 lines)
     - Similar structure but **requires using the user's selected models**
     - Emphasis: "MUST use EXACTLY these models - Do NOT change this model under any circumstances"
     - Dynamic template: Fills in user's selected models into the prompt
     - Removes model selection logic entirely

#### **model-suggester/utils/polling.ts** (247 lines)
- **Purpose**: Polling utility for predictions (server-side)
- **Exports**:
  - `pollPrediction(predictionId, options)`: Async function that polls for prediction completion
  - Types: `PredictionStatus`, `PredictionResponse`, `PollOptions`

- **Configuration Options**:
  - `maxAttempts`: 20 (default)
  - `initialDelayMs`: 2000 (default)
  - `maxDelayMs`: 8000 (default)
  - `totalTimeoutMs`: 45 seconds (default)
  - `fixedInterval`: false (uses exponential backoff by default)
  - `provider`: "replicate" (default)
  - `baseUrl`: Server URL for internal API fallback

- **Behavior**:
  - Polls Replicate API directly (when provider === "replicate")
  - Falls back to internal API route for other providers
  - Uses exponential backoff or fixed interval
  - Returns immediately on "succeeded" or "failed" status
  - Extensive debug logging

### 3. **How Tools Are Currently Defined and Exported**

**Flow**:
```
model-suggester/tools.ts
  ├─ Each tool uses ai.tool() function
  ├─ Named exports: generateImageTool, generateVideoTool, ...
  └─ Each tool:
      ├─ Has `description` string
      ├─ Has `inputSchema` (Zod object)
      └─ Has `execute` async function

media-agent/index.ts
  ├─ Imports all 7 tools from tools.ts
  ├─ Creates mediaAgentTools object
  │   └─ Maps tool exports to keys: { generateImage, generateVideo, ... }
  └─ Passes mediaAgentTools to ToolLoopAgent constructor
```

### 4. **Agent Configuration (ToolLoopAgent Pattern)**

**Initialization** (media-agent/index.ts:45-107):
```typescript
new ToolLoopAgent<MediaAgentCallOptions, typeof mediaAgentTools>({
  id: "media-agent",
  model: anthropic("claude-haiku-4-5"),
  tools: mediaAgentTools,
  maxOutputTokens: 10_000,
  stopWhen: stepCountIs(20),
  callOptionsSchema: mediaAgentCallOptionsSchema,
  experimental_telemetry: { isEnabled: true },
  prepareCall: async ({ options, model, tools, maxOutputTokens, prompt }) => {
    // Dynamic prompt selection based on isAutoMode flag
    // Returns merged instruction set
  }
})
```

**prepareCall Hook Details**:
- Called before each agent execution
- Receives call parameters and options
- **Critical**: Must return `{ model, tools, maxOutputTokens, instructions, prompt }`
- **Key Logic**:
  1. Check `options.isAutoMode` (default true)
  2. If manual: fetch `manual_mode_agent_prompt(imageModel, videoModel)`
  3. If auto: fetch `main_agent_prompt`
  4. Merge with optional `globalSystemInstructions`
  5. Return with modified `instructions` field

### 5. **Integration Points**

**API Route** (`src/app/api/agents/v1/route.ts`):
- Receives: user message, projectId, chatId, modelSelection options, globalSystemInstructions
- Validates messages against tool schemas
- Creates `agentOptions: MediaAgentCallOptions` from client request
- Calls `createAgentUIStreamResponse({ agent: mediaAgent, uiMessages, options: agentOptions, ... })`
- Saves messages to PostgreSQL (user immediately, assistant in onFinish)

### 6. **Current Limitations/Issues with Modularity**

**Identified Problems**:

1. **Tool Definition Location**: All 7 tools crammed in single `model-suggester/tools.ts` file (222 lines) - harder to maintain as more tools are added

2. **Prompt Coupling**: Prompts are directly in `model-suggester/prompts.ts` and imported in `media-agent/index.ts` - makes them harder to find and modify

3. **No Tool Categories**: Tools aren't organized by function (generation tools vs. editing tools vs. processing tools)

4. **Polling Logic**: Buried in `utils/polling.ts` - not obvious it's only for Replicate

5. **Model Metadata Scattered**: Model lists appear in:
   - `prompts.ts` (vision_prompt, main_agent_prompt)
   - Individual tool schemas (aspect_ratio enums, model literals)
   - No single source of truth for available models

6. **Server Actions Tightly Coupled**: Each tool directly imports and calls specific server actions - no abstraction layer

7. **Missing Tool Validation**: No schema validation or documentation of tool inputs/outputs separate from Zod schemas

### 7. **Recommended Modular Structure** (for your refactoring)

```
src/lib/agents/
├── media-agent/
│   ├── index.ts              # Main agent export
│   ├── types.ts              # Call options schema
│   └── hooks/
│       └── prepareCall.ts    # Dynamic prompt logic (moved out)
│
├── tools/
│   ├── index.ts              # Export all tools
│   ├── generation/
│   │   ├── generate-image.ts
│   │   ├── generate-video.ts
│   │   └── generate-audio.ts
│   ├── editing/
│   │   └── edit-image.ts
│   └── processing/
│       ├── remove-background.ts
│       ├── resize-image.ts
│       └── upscale-image.ts
│
├── prompts/
│   ├── index.ts              # Export all prompts
│   ├── auto-mode.ts
│   ├── manual-mode.ts
│   ├── vision-router.ts
│   └── constants/
│       └── models.ts         # Single source of truth for models
│
├── utils/
│   ├── polling.ts            # Keep as-is
│   └── tool-helpers.ts       # Shared tool utilities
│
└── constants/
    └── tool-limits.ts        # Max calls, rate limits, etc.
```

This structure would:
- **Separate concerns**: Each tool in its own file
- **Centralize models**: Single file for all model metadata
- **Improve discoverability**: Clear folder organization
- **Enable parallel development**: Teams can work on different tools independently
- **Ease testing**: Individual tool testing without loading entire suite
- **Better documentation**: Each tool file can have detailed comments

---

## Summary

The current implementation uses a **ToolLoopAgent** pattern from Vercel AI SDK v6+ with:
- **7 tools** for media generation/editing (all in one file)
- **Dynamic prompt selection** based on auto/manual mode
- **Langfuse integration** for observability
- **PostgreSQL persistence** for chat history
- **Client-side polling** for async operations

The main area for refactoring is **organization**: tools, prompts, and model metadata are distributed across files without clear boundaries. A more modular structure would improve maintainability and scalability as the agent feature set grows.

Design a detailed implementation plan for refactoring the agents folder to adopt a modular structure.

## Current State (PR #19 - User's branch)
```
src/lib/agents/
├── media-agent/
│   ├── index.ts              # ToolLoopAgent + 7 tools imported
│   └── types.ts              # MediaAgentCallOptions schema
└── model-suggester/
    ├── tools.ts              # 7 tool definitions (222 lines)
    ├── prompts.ts            # System prompts (auto & manual modes)
    ├── model.md              # Model docs
    └── utils/
        └── polling.ts        # Prediction polling
```

## Target Structure (from PR #11 - Teammate's approach)
```
src/lib/agents/
├── index.ts                    # Clean exports
├── media-agent.ts              # ToolLoopAgent definition
├── prompts.ts                  # System prompts
├── types.ts                    # Shared types
├── middleware/
│   ├── error-handler.ts        # Error handling utilities
│   └── tracing.ts              # Langfuse tracing abstraction
├── tools/
│   ├── index.ts                # Tool exports
│   ├── generate-image.ts
│   ├── generate-video.ts
│   ├── edit-image.ts
│   ├── generate-audio.ts
│   ├── remove-background.ts
│   ├── resize-image.ts
│   └── upscale-image.ts
└── utils/
    ├── polling.ts
    └── stream-utils.ts
```

## Requirements to Keep from User's PR
1. Tool naming: generateImage, generateVideo, editImage (NOT generateImageWithPolling)
2. ToolLoopAgent pattern with prepareCall hook
3. createAgentUIStreamResponse usage in route.ts
4. Agent name: mediaAgent (not creativeAgent)
5. MediaAgentCallOptions with isAutoMode, imageModel, videoModel, globalSystemInstructions
6. Bug fix for tool parts persistence (in chatMessage.repo.ts - ALREADY DONE)
7. CLAUDE.md documentation (ALREADY EXISTS)

## Requirements to Adopt from PR #11
1. Separate tool files in tools/ folder (one file per tool)
2. middleware/ folder with error-handler.ts and tracing.ts
3. utils/ folder with stream-utils.ts (extractTextFromUiMessage, etc.)
4. Clean index.ts at root level for exports
5. Flat prompts.ts at root level (not nested in model-suggester)
6. types.ts at root level

## Route.ts Changes Needed
Currently has these inline functions that should move to utils/stream-utils.ts:
- extractTextFromUiMessage()
- extractTextFromParts()
- extractLatestUserTextFromUiMessages()
- summarizeParts()

Also update imports to use new paths.

## Plan the refactoring in phases:
1. Phase 1: Create new folder structure (tools/, middleware/, utils/)
2. Phase 2: Split tools.ts into individual tool files
3. Phase 3: Create middleware files (error-handler, tracing)
4. Phase 4: Move utility functions to stream-utils.ts
5. Phase 5: Reorganize agent and prompts at root level
6. Phase 6: Update all imports (route.ts, other consumers)
7. Phase 7: Delete old model-suggester folder
8. Phase 8: Verify and test

Provide specific file-by-file changes with code snippets where helpful.

content.content:
1→import { anthropic } from "@ai-sdk/anthropic";
     2→import { type InferAgentUIMessage, stepCountIs, ToolLoopAgent } from "ai";
     3→
     4→import {
     5→  removeBackgroundTool,
     6→  editImageTool,
     7→  generateAudioTool,
     8→  generateImageTool,
     9→  generateVideoTool,
    10→  resizeImageTool,
    11→  upscaleImageTool,
    12→} from "@/lib/agents/model-suggester/tools";
    13→import {
    14→  main_agent_prompt,
    15→  manual_mode_agent_prompt,
    16→} from "@/lib/langfuse/prompt";
    17→import { getPromptWithFallback } from "@/lib/langfuse/prompt-fetcher";
    18→import logger from "@/utils/logger";
    19→
    20→import {
    21→  type MediaAgentCallOptions,
    22→  mediaAgentCallOptionsSchema,
    23→} from "./types";
    24→
    25→/**
    26→ * Tools available to the media agent.
    27→ * Exported for validation in the route handler.
    28→ */
    29→export const mediaAgentTools = {
    30→  generateImage: generateImageTool,
    31→  generateVideo: generateVideoTool,
    32→  editImage: editImageTool,
    33→  generateAudio: generateAudioTool,
    34→  removeBackground: removeBackgroundTool,
    35→  resizeImage: resizeImageTool,
    36→  upscaleImage: upscaleImageTool,
    37→};
    38→
    39→/**
    40→ * Media Agent - A specialized AI creative assistant for image generation,
    41→ * video creation, audio production, and media editing tasks.
    42→ *
    43→ * Uses ToolLoopAgent pattern for cleaner architecture and built-in loop control.
    44→ */
    45→export const mediaAgent = new ToolLoopAgent<
    46→  MediaAgentCallOptions,
    47→  typeof mediaAgentTools
    48→>({
    49→  id: "media-agent",
    50→  model: anthropic("claude-haiku-4-5"),
    51→  tools: mediaAgentTools,
    52→  maxOutputTokens: 10_000,
    53→  stopWhen: stepCountIs(20),
    54→  callOptionsSchema: mediaAgentCallOptionsSchema,
    55→  experimental_telemetry: { isEnabled: true },
    56→
    57→  /**
    58→   * Prepare the call with dynamic system prompt based on mode (auto vs manual).
    59→   * Receives default settings and returns overrides for this call.
    60→   * IMPORTANT: Must pass through `prompt` from input to output (SDK uses prompt, not messages).
    61→   */
    62→  prepareCall: async ({ options, model, tools, maxOutputTokens, prompt }) => {
    63→    const isManualMode = options && !options.isAutoMode;
    64→
    65→    let baseSystemPrompt: string;
    66→
    67→    if (isManualMode && options) {
    68→      // Manual mode: use user's selected models
    69→      const manualPromptFallback = manual_mode_agent_prompt(
    70→        options.imageModel,
    71→        options.videoModel,
    72→      );
    73→      baseSystemPrompt = await getPromptWithFallback(
    74→        "manual_mode_agent_prompt",
    75→        manualPromptFallback,
    76→      );
    77→      logger.info(
    78→        `Manual mode enabled - Image: ${options.imageModel}, Video: ${options.videoModel}`,
    79→      );
    80→    } else {
    81→      // Auto mode: agent selects best models
    82→      baseSystemPrompt = await getPromptWithFallback(
    83→        "main_agent_prompt",
    84→        main_agent_prompt,
    85→      );
    86→      logger.info("Auto mode enabled - agent will select best models");
    87→    }
    88→
    89→    // Merge with user's custom system instructions if provided
    90→    const globalInstructions = options?.globalSystemInstructions?.trim();
    91→    const mergedInstructions = [
    92→      baseSystemPrompt,
    93→      globalInstructions?.length
    94→        ? `\n\n<user-system-instructions>\n${globalInstructions}\n</user-system-instructions>\n`
    95→        : "",
    96→    ].join("");
    97→
    98→    // Return the updated settings - pass through required fields including prompt
    99→    // NOTE: createAgentUIStreamResponse passes model messages as `prompt`, not `messages`
   100→    return {
   101→      model,
   102→      tools,
   103→      maxOutputTokens,
   104→      instructions: mergedInstructions,
   105→      prompt, // CRITICAL: Must pass through prompt from createAgentUIStreamResponse
   106→    };
   107→  },
   108→});
   109→
   110→/**
   111→ * Type-safe UIMessage type for the media agent.
   112→ * Use this with useChat<MediaAgentUIMessage>() for type-safe tool results.
   113→ */
   114→export type MediaAgentUIMessage = InferAgentUIMessage<typeof mediaAgent>;
   115→
   116→// Re-export types for convenience
   117→export type { MediaAgentCallOptions } from "./types";
   118→

1→import { z } from "zod";
     2→
     3→/**
     4→ * Schema for media agent call options.
     5→ * These options control how the agent behaves for each call.
     6→ */
     7→export const mediaAgentCallOptionsSchema = z.object({
     8→  /**
     9→   * When true, the agent automatically selects the best image/video models.
    10→   * When false, uses the user's manually selected models.
    11→   */
    12→  isAutoMode: z.boolean().default(true),
    13→
    14→  /**
    15→   * The image model to use when isAutoMode is false.
    16→   * Ignored when isAutoMode is true.
    17→   */
    18→  imageModel: z.string().nullable().default(null),
    19→
    20→  /**
    21→   * The video model to use when isAutoMode is false.
    22→   * Ignored when isAutoMode is true.
    23→   */
    24→  videoModel: z.string().nullable().default(null),
    25→
    26→  /**
    27→   * Optional custom system instructions provided by the user.
    28→   * These are appended to the base system prompt.
    29→   */
    30→  globalSystemInstructions: z.string().optional(),
    31→});
    32→
    33→/**
    34→ * Type for media agent call options, inferred from the Zod schema.
    35→ */
    36→export type MediaAgentCallOptions = z.infer<typeof mediaAgentCallOptionsSchema>;
    37→
